headerDisplay: Control Neewer Bluetooth LE lights from Apple Home. Each light is matched by its advertised Bluetooth name and/or its peripheral id, then exposed as a HomeKit lightbulb with on/off, brightness, color temperature, and (for RGB models) hue/saturation.
footerDisplay: Run Homebridge with `debug: true` once to log every Neewer peripheral the host sees (name + id), then copy the values into each light's `bleName` / `bleId`.

oneOf.title: Mock (testing in Apple Home, no hardware)

scanTimeoutSeconds.title: Scan timeout (seconds)
scanTimeoutSeconds.description: How long to actively scan for lights on startup. 0 = scan continuously until all configured lights are found.

writeDebounceMs.title: Write debounce (ms)
writeDebounceMs.description: Coalesce rapid HomeKit slider changes into a single BLE write.

keepAliveSeconds.title: Keep-alive interval (seconds)
keepAliveSeconds.description: Re-assert connection / re-scan for dropped lights at this interval. 0 disables.

bleName.title: Bluetooth name match
bleName.description: Case-insensitive substring of the advertised name, e.g. 'RGB660 PRO' or 'NEEWER-RGB176'. Leave blank if matching only by id.

bleId.title: Peripheral id (optional, exact)
bleId.description: The host-specific peripheral UUID (macOS) shown in debug logs. Use this to disambiguate multiple identical lights.
